# commands/ — Context for Claude

(Scoped to this directory only. See root CLAUDE.md for project-wide context.)

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Architecture

The 7 top-level `.md` files are Claude Code slash commands. They are not called by each other directly — `pipeline.md` reads reference files from `references/pipeline/` and fills `[VARIABLES]` to build subagent prompts at runtime. `pipeline-team.md` does the same with `references/pipeline-team/`. The standalone commands (`spec.md`, `impl-plan.md`, `impl.md`, `audit-implementation.md`, `fix.md`) are also read wholesale by `pipeline.md`'s Phase 1 and by the reference templates — they serve double duty as both user-facing commands and subagent instruction sources.

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Invariants

1. **Line count matters.** Several specs and audits have enforced line-count targets on `pipeline.md` and `pipeline-team.md`. Edits that add content may trigger audit failures in downstream pipeline runs if they push files past their targets. Check `learnings.md` for past instances of this.
2. **Reference files use bracket variables, not template literals.** Placeholders like `[WORKTREE_PATH]`, `[SPEC_FILENAME]`, `[MASTER_REPO_PATH]` are filled by the orchestrator (pipeline.md) before passing to subagents. Using `$VARIABLE` or `${VARIABLE}` syntax will not be substituted — it will be passed literally to the subagent.
3. **`phase6-merge.md` checks for `package.json` before invoking patch-notes.** If `package.json` doesn't exist at the project root (as is the case for this project currently), the patch-notes step is silently skipped. Adding a `package.json` will activate automatic version bumping on every merge.

<!-- generated by init-claude-md -->
## Gotchas

1. **`phase3-impl.md` overrides impl.md's Phase 7 and Phase 8.** The reference template tells the subagent to commit automatically (skip the user prompt) and skip the merge phase. An edit to `impl.md` Phase 7/8 behavior won't affect pipeline-driven runs — only standalone `/impl` invocations.
2. **`phase5-reaudit.md` appends to the existing audit file** rather than creating a new one. The re-audit output is written into `[AUDIT_FILENAME]` as an addendum. Code that expects one audit = one file will see both the original audit and the re-audit in the same document.
